Steps.Name: Open Process
Steps.Description: Obtains a handle to the target process. Required before any cross-process memory or thread operations can be performed.

Parameters.Name: dwDesiredAccess
Parameters.Description: Requested access rights for the target process handle, determining what operations the caller can perform — such as reading/writing process memory, creating threads, or terminating the process.

Parameters.Name: bInheritHandle
Parameters.Description: Whether child processes spawned by the caller can inherit this handle. Set to FALSE — no handle inheritance is needed for injection.

Parameters.Name: dwProcessId
Parameters.Description: The PID of the target process to open. Supplied externally by the caller as the injection target.

Steps.Name: Virtual Alloc Ex
Steps.Description: Allocates a region of memory inside the target process's address space where the payload will be written and later executed.

Parameters.Name: hProcess
Parameters.Description: Handle to the target process obtained from OpenProcess. Determines where the allocation occurs.

Parameters.Name: lpAddress
Parameters.Description: Desired starting address for the allocation. NULL lets the OS choose a suitable address.

Parameters.Name: dwSize
Parameters.Description: Size in bytes of the region to allocate. Matches the payload length to fit it exactly.

Parameters.Name: flAllocationType
Parameters.Description: MEM_RESERVE reserves the address range; MEM_COMMIT backs it with physical memory. Both flags together make the region immediately usable.

Parameters.Name: flProtect
Parameters.Description: Memory protection for the allocated region. PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE allows the payload to be written and then executed directly — a strong indicator of code injection.

Steps.Name: Write Process Memory
Steps.Description: Copies the shellcode payload from the caller's address space into the allocated region inside the target process.

Parameters.Name: hProcess
Parameters.Description: Handle to the target process. Determines which process's memory is written to.

Parameters.Name: lpBaseAddress
Parameters.Description: Destination address inside the target process — the buffer returned by VirtualAllocEx.

Parameters.Name: lpBuffer
Parameters.Description: Pointer to the source payload in the caller's memory that will be copied into the target.

Parameters.Name: nSize
Parameters.Description: Number of bytes to write. Matches the full payload length.

Parameters.Name: lpNumberOfBytesWritten
Parameters.Description: Optional output for how many bytes were actually written. NULL means the caller is not checking this.

Steps.Name: Create Remote Thread
Steps.Description: Spawns a new thread inside the target process with its entry point set to the injected payload, triggering execution of the shellcode.

Parameters.Name: hProcess
Parameters.Description: Handle to the target process where the new thread will be created.

Parameters.Name: lpThreadAttributes
Parameters.Description: Security attributes for the thread handle. NULL uses default security — no special inheritance or descriptor.

Parameters.Name: dwStackSize
Parameters.Description: Initial stack size for the new thread. 0 uses the executable's default stack size.

Parameters.Name: lpStartAddress
Parameters.Description: Entry point of the new thread — cast to the allocated remote buffer, so the thread begins executing the injected payload.

Parameters.Name: lpParameter
Parameters.Description: Argument passed to the thread function. NULL — no parameter is needed for this payload.

Parameters.Name: dwCreationFlags
Parameters.Description: Thread creation flags. 0 means the thread starts running immediately after creation.

Parameters.Name: lpThreadId
Parameters.Description: Optional output for the new thread's ID. NULL — the caller does not need to track the thread ID.

Steps.Name: Close Thread Handle
Steps.Description: Releases the thread handle. The injected thread continues running — this only closes the caller's reference to it.

Parameters.Name: hObject
Parameters.Description: The remote thread handle returned by CreateRemoteThread.

Steps.Name: Close Process Handle
Steps.Description: Releases the process handle, completing cleanup. The injected thread remains running inside the target process.

Parameters.Name: hObject
Parameters.Description: The target process handle obtained from OpenProcess.
